info.title: Chat App API
info.description: Chat applications support session persistence, allowing previous chat history to be used as context for responses. This can be applicable for chatbot, customer service AI, etc.

servers.description: The base URL for the Chat App API. Replace {api_base_url} with the actual API base URL provided for your application.

api_base_url.description: Actual base URL of the API

post.description: Send a request to the chat application.

requestBody.description: Request body to send a chat message.

value.query: What are the specs of the iPhone 13 Pro Max?
What are the specs of the iPhone 13 Pro Max?

200.description:
Successful response. The content type and structure depend on the `response_mode` parameter in the request.

- If `response_mode` is `blocking`, returns `application/json` with a `ChatCompletionResponse` object.
- If `response_mode` is `streaming`, returns `text/event-stream` with a stream of `ChunkChatEvent` objects.

value.answer: iPhone 13 Pro Max specs are listed here:...

retriever_resources.content: "Model","Release Date","Display Size","Resolution","Processor","RAM","Storage","Camera","Battery","Operating System" "iPhone 13 Pro Max","September 24, 2021","6.7 inch","1284 x 2778","Hexa-core (2x3.23 GHz Avalanche + 4x1.82 GHz Blizzard)","6 GB","128, 256, 512 GB, 1TB","12 MP","4352 mAh","iOS 15"

schema.description:
A stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE). Each event is a JSON object prefixed with `data: ` and terminated by two newlines. See `ChunkChatEvent` for possible event structures.

**SSE Parsing Guide:** Each event is a line prefixed with `data: ` followed by a JSON object, terminated by `\n\n`. Strip the `data: ` prefix before parsing JSON. The `event` field inside the JSON determines the event type. The stream ends when a `message_end` event is received. Ignore `ping` events (sent every 10 seconds to keep the connection alive). If an `error` event is received mid-stream, the stream terminates — parse the error object for details. Note that the HTTP status code is always `200` even when an error event occurs within the stream.

streamingResponseAgent.value: data: {"event": "agent_thought", "id": "agent_thought_id_1", "task_id": "task123", "message_id": "msg123", "conversation_id": "conv123", "position": 1, "thought": "Thinking about calling a tool...", "tool": "dalle3", "tool_input": "{\"dalle3\": {\"prompt\": \"a cute cat\"}}", "created_at": 1705395332} data: {"event": "message_file", "id": "file_id_1", "type": "image", "belongs_to": "assistant", "url": "https://example.com/cat.png", "conversation_id": "conv123"} data: {"event": "agent_message", "task_id": "task123", "message_id": "msg123", "conversation_id": "conv123", "answer": "Here is the image: ", "created_at": 1705395333} data: {"event": "message_end", "task_id":"task123", "message_id": "msg123", "conversation_id": "conv123", "metadata": {"usage": {"total_tokens": 50, "latency": 2.5}}}

400.description:
- `app_unavailable` : App unavailable or misconfigured.
- `not_chat_app` : App mode does not match the API route.
- `conversation_completed` : The conversation has ended.
- `provider_not_initialize` : No valid model provider credentials found.
- `provider_quota_exceeded` : Model provider quota exhausted.
- `model_currently_not_support` : Current model unavailable.
- `completion_request_error` : Text generation failed.

value.message: App unavailable, please check your app configurations.
Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.
The conversation has ended. Please start a new conversation.
No valid model provider credentials found. Please go to Settings -> Model Provider to complete your provider credentials.
Your quota for Dify Hosted OpenAI has been exhausted. Please go to Settings -> Model Provider to complete your own provider credentials.
Dify Hosted OpenAI trial currently not support the GPT-4 model.

429.description:
- `too_many_requests` : Too many concurrent requests for this app.
- `rate_limit_error` : The upstream model provider rate limit was exceeded.

value.message: Too many requests. Please try again later.

post.description: Stops a chat message generation task. Only supported in `streaming` mode.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Task ID, can be obtained from the streaming chunk return of the [Send Chat Message](/api-reference/chats/send-chat-message) API.

user.description: User identifier, must be consistent with the user passed in the send message interface.

400.description: `not_chat_app` : App mode does not match the API route.

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.

How does the battery compare to iPhone 12?

400.description:
- `not_chat_app` : App mode does not match the API route.
- `bad_request` : Suggested questions feature is disabled.

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.

post.description: Upload a file for use when sending messages, enabling multimodal understanding of images, documents, audio, and video. Uploaded files are for use by the current end-user only.

file.description: The file to be uploaded. Supported types include images, documents, audio, and video.

user.description: User identifier, defined by the developer's rules, must be unique within the application.

400.description:
- `no_file_uploaded` : No file was provided in the request.
- `too_many_files` : Only one file is allowed per request.
- `filename_not_exists_error` : The uploaded file has no filename.

value.message: The specified filename does not exist.

get.description: Preview or download uploaded files previously uploaded via the [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file) API. Files can only be accessed if they belong to messages within the requesting application.

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the file to preview, obtained from the [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file) API response.

parameters.name: as_attachment
parameters.description: If `true`, forces the file to download as an attachment instead of previewing in browser.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: User identifier, used for end-user context.

200.description: Returns the raw file content. The `Content-Type` header is set to the file's MIME type. If `as_attachment` is `true`, the file is returned as a download with `Content-Disposition: attachment`.

403.description: `file_access_denied` : Access to the requested file is denied.

value.message: Access to the requested file is denied.

404.description: `file_not_found` : The requested file was not found.

value.message: The requested file was not found.

get.description: Retrieve an end user by ID. Useful when other APIs return an end-user ID (e.g., `created_by` from [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file)).

post.description: Submit feedback for a message. End users can rate messages as `like` or `dislike`, and optionally provide text feedback. Pass `null` for `rating` to revoke previously submitted feedback.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all feedback submitted for messages in this application, including both end-user and admin feedback.

data.content: The response accurately answered my question about product specifications.
The answer was too vague and did not address the specific pricing question.

get.description: Retrieve the conversation list for the current user, ordered by most recently active.

parameters.name: last_id
parameters.description: The ID of the last record on the current page (for pagination).

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sorting field. Use '-' prefix for descending order.

data.introduction: Welcome! How can I help you today?

value.message: Please check if your app mode matches the right API route.

get.description: Returns historical chat records in a scrolling load format, with the first page returning the latest `limit` messages, i.e., in reverse order.

parameters.name: first_id
parameters.description: The ID of the first chat record on the current page. Default is `null` (fetches the latest messages). For subsequent pages, use the ID of the first message from the current list to get older messages.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of chat history messages to return per request.

post.description: Convert audio file to text. Supported formats: `mp3`, `mp4`, `mpeg`, `mpga`, `m4a`, `wav`, `webm`. File size limit is `15 MB`.

- `app_unavailable` : App unavailable or misconfigured.

- `provider_not_support_speech_to_text` : Model provider does not support speech-to-text.

post.description: Creates a new annotation. Annotations provide predefined question-answer pairs that the app can match and return directly instead of generating a response.

value.question: What is Dify?
value.answer: Dify is an open-source LLM application development platform.

value.answer: Dify is an open-source LLM application development platform.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Keyword to filter annotations by question or answer content.

parameters.name: annotation_id

delete.description: Deletes an annotation and its associated hit history.

parameters.name: annotation_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the annotation to delete.

post.description: Enables or disables the annotation reply feature. Requires embedding model configuration when enabling. Executes asynchronously — use [Retrieve Annotation Reply Job Status](/api-reference/annotations/get-annotation-reply-job-status) to track progress.

get.description: Retrieves the status of an asynchronous annotation reply configuration job started by [Configure Annotation Reply](/api-reference/annotations/configure-annotation-reply).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action type, must match the [Configure Annotation Reply](/api-reference/annotations/configure-annotation-reply) call.

400.description: `invalid_param` : The specified job does not exist.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API Key authentication. For all API requests, include your API Key in the `Authorization` HTTP Header, prefixed with `Bearer `. Example: `Authorization: Bearer {API_KEY}`. **Strongly recommend storing your API Key on the server-side, not shared or stored on the client-side, to avoid possible API-Key leakage that can lead to serious consequences.**

inputs.description: Allows the entry of various variable values defined by the App. Contains key/value pairs. Refer to the `user_input_form` field in the [Get App Parameters](/api-reference/applications/get-app-parameters) response to discover the variable names and types expected by your app.

response_mode.description: Mode of response return. `streaming` (recommended) uses SSE. `blocking` returns after completion (may be interrupted for long processes; not supported in Agent Assistant mode). Cloudflare timeout is `100 s`.

user.description: User identifier, unique within the application. This identifier scopes data access — conversations, messages, and files are only visible when queried with the same `user` value.

conversation_id.description: Conversation ID to continue a conversation. Pass the previous message's `conversation_id`. To start a new conversation, omit this field or pass an empty string. The response will return a `conversation_id` — pass it in subsequent messages to continue that conversation.

files.description: File list for multimodal understanding, including images, documents, audio, and video. To attach a local file, first upload it via [Upload File](/api-reference/files/upload-file) and use the returned `id` as `upload_file_id` with `transfer_method: local_file`.

transfer_method.description: Transfer method: `remote_url` for file URL, `local_file` for uploaded file.

upload_file_id.description: Uploaded file ID obtained from the [File Upload](/api-reference/files/upload-file) API (required when `transfer_method` is `local_file`).

auto_generate_name.description: Auto-generate conversation title. If `false`, use the [Rename Conversation](/api-reference/conversations/rename-conversation) API with `auto_generate: true` for async title generation.

task_id.description: Task ID for request tracking and stop response API.

id.description: Unique ID of this response event.

message_id.description: Unique message ID. Use this as the `message_id` parameter when calling feedback or suggested questions endpoints.

metadata.description: Metadata including usage and retriever resources.

ChunkChatEvent.description: Base schema for Server-Sent Event chunks in streaming mode.

allOf.description: TTS audio stream event (base64 encoded Mp3). Available if auto-play enabled.

audio.description: Base64-encoded MP3 audio chunk. Decode and concatenate all chunks in order to produce a complete audio file.
Empty string. Signals the end of the audio stream.

allOf.description: Agent thought, LLM thinking, tool call details (Agent mode).

position.description: Position of this thought in the sequence for the message.

tool.description: List of tools called, split by `;`.

tool_input.description: Input of tools in JSON format.

message_files.description: File IDs of files related to this thought.

allOf.description: Message file event, a new file created by a tool.

belongs_to.description: Who this file belongs to. Always `assistant` for tool-generated files.

allOf.description: Message end event, streaming has ended.

metadata.description: Metadata including usage and retriever resources.

allOf.description: Message content replacement event (e.g., due to content moderation).
Ping event to keep connection alive.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt.

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the completion.

RetrieverResource.description: Citation and attribution information for a retriever resource.

id.description: Unique ID of the retriever resource.

message_id.description: ID of the message this resource belongs to.

position.description: Position of the resource in the list.

segment_id.description: ID of the specific chunk within the document.

hit_count.description: Number of times this chunk was hit.

segment_position.description: Position of the chunk within the document.

created_by.description: ID of the user who uploaded the file.

type.description: End user type. Always `service_api` for Service API users.

external_user_id.description: The `user` identifier provided in API requests (e.g., the `user` field in [Send Chat Message](/api-reference/chats/send-chat-message)).

is_anonymous.description: Whether the user is anonymous. `true` when no `user` identifier was provided in the original API request.

session_id.description: Session identifier. Defaults to the `external_user_id` value.

MessageFeedbackRequest.description: Request body for submitting message feedback.

rating.description: Feedback rating. Set to `null` to revoke previously submitted feedback.

user.description: User identifier, defined by the developer, must ensure uniqueness within the application.

content.description: Optional text feedback providing additional detail.

rating.description: Feedback rating. `like` for positive, `dislike` for negative.

from_source.description: Feedback source. `user` for end-user feedback submitted via API, `admin` for feedback submitted from the console.

from_end_user_id.description: End user ID who submitted the feedback. Present when `from_source` is `user`.

from_account_id.description: Account ID who submitted the feedback. Present when `from_source` is `admin`.

ConversationMessageItem.description: A single message in a conversation.

parent_message_id.description: Parent message ID for threaded conversations.

status.description: Message status. `normal` for successful messages, `error` when generation failed.

rating.description: Feedback rating. `like` for positive, `dislike` for negative.

retriever_resources.description: Retriever resources used for this message.

extra_contents.description: Additional execution content associated with this message, such as human input form data.

MessageFileItem.description: A file attached to a message.

transfer_method.description: Transfer method used. `remote_url` for URL-based files, `local_file` for uploaded files, `tool_file` for tool-generated files.

belongs_to.description: Who this file belongs to. `user` for user-uploaded files, `assistant` for assistant-generated files.

upload_file_id.description: Upload file ID if transferred via `local_file`.

AgentThoughtItem.description: An agent thought step in the message.

message_id.description: Unique message ID this thought belongs to.

tool_input.description: Input of tools in JSON format.

files.description: File IDs related to this thought.

ConversationRenameRequest.description: Request body for renaming a conversation.

name.description: The name of the conversation. Required when `auto_generate` is `false`.

auto_generate.description: Automatically generate the conversation name. When `true`, the `name` field is ignored.

has_more.description: Whether there is a next page.

value.description: Variable value (can be a JSON string for complex types).

ConversationVariableUpdateRequest.description: Request body for updating a conversation variable.

value.description: The new value for the variable. Must match the variable's expected type.

TextToAudioRequest.description: Request body for text-to-audio conversion. Provide either `message_id` or `text`.

message_id.description: Message ID. Takes priority over `text` when both are provided.

voice.description: Voice to use for text-to-speech. Available voices depend on the TTS provider configured for this app. Use the `voice` value from [Get App Parameters](/api-reference/applications/get-app-parameters) → `text_to_speech.voice` for the default.

mode.description: Application mode. `chat` for basic chat apps, `agent-chat` for agent-based apps, `advanced-chat` for chatflow apps.

opening_statement.description: Opening statement displayed at conversation start.

suggested_questions_after_answer.description: Configuration for suggested questions after answer.

autoPlay.description: Auto-play setting. `enabled` to auto-play audio, `disabled` to require manual play.

user_input_form.description: List of user input form elements.

image_file_size_limit.description: Maximum image file size in MB.

audio_file_size_limit.description: Maximum audio file size in MB.

video_file_size_limit.description: Maximum video file size in MB.

workflow_file_upload_limit.description: Maximum number of files for workflow file upload.

options.description: List of selectable values for this form control.

tool_icons.description: Tool icons. Keys are tool names.

ToolIconDetail.title: Emoji Icon
ToolIconDetail.description: Detail of a tool icon using emoji.

chat_color_theme_inverted.description: Whether the chat color theme is inverted.

icon_type.description: Type of icon used. `emoji` for emoji icons, `image` for uploaded image icons.

use_icon_as_answer_icon.description: Whether to use the app icon as the answer icon.

data.description: List of annotation items for the current page.

has_more.description: `true` if more pages are available beyond the current result set.

total.description: Total number of annotations matching the query.

question.description: Question text that triggers this annotation.

answer.description: Predefined answer returned when the annotation is matched.

hit_count.description: Number of times this annotation has been matched and returned as a reply.

CreateAnnotationRequest.description: Request body for creating a new annotation.

UpdateAnnotationRequest.description: Request body for updating an annotation.

InitialAnnotationReplySettingsRequest.description: Request body for configuring annotation reply settings.

embedding_provider_name.description: Name of the embedding model provider (e.g., `openai`, `cohere`).

embedding_model_name.description: Name of the embedding model to use for annotation matching (e.g., `text-embedding-3-small`).

score_threshold.description: Minimum similarity score for an annotation to be considered a match. Higher values require closer matches.

job_id.description: Asynchronous job ID. Use with [Retrieve Annotation Reply Job Status](/api-reference/annotations/get-annotation-reply-job-status) to track progress.

job_status.description: Current job status. `waiting` for queued, `processing` for in progress, `completed` when finished, `error` if failed.

job_id.description: Job ID from the [Configure Annotation Reply](/api-reference/annotations/configure-annotation-reply) call.

job_status.description: Current job status. `waiting` for queued, `processing` for in progress, `completed` when finished, `error` if failed.

error_msg.description: Error message describing why the job failed. Empty string when `job_status` is not `error`.
